title: Symbolism Did Kaoru break Shinjis heart
display_title: Symbolism Did Kaoru break Shinjis heart
wikitext:
[]: Symbolism: Did Kaoru break Shinji's heart?             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:00 pm During that pause as Eva 01 holds Kaoru, a prog knife is clearly protruding from his chest, the heart perhaps? I think you know where this is going...

[]: Re: Symbolism: Did Kaoru break Shinji's heart?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:14 pm InvaderRen wrote: During that pause as Eva 01 holds Kaworu, a prog knife is clearly protruding from her chest, the heart perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:25 pm It didnt have to literally piece her heart to have the intended effect... That knife could have been placed anywhere else on her body, but Anno chose the heart. What could it mean? Homo-Kaoru breaking Shinji's poor teenage heart? Injuring what was thought to be the emotional center of the body, perhaps foreshadowing Shinji's indifference and perversion in EoE? _________ As for Gendo's Groping Adventure, I personally feel that he was going Rei's kidneys, because, if i remember rite, it was eithers Hebrews or Kabblists that believed that the kidneys where the emotional center (as we associate with the heart today)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:49 pm InvaderRen wrote: What could it mean? Femme-Kaworu breaking Shinji's poor teenage heart?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 6:58 pm Can somebody find out the time between the stabbing and that line? I currently dont have my Platinum Collection?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 7:11 pm InvaderRen wrote: Can somebody find out the time between the stabbing and that line? I currently dont have my Platinum Collection?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 7:47 pm what are your opinions? and on a side note, Ornette, you go to Carnegie Mellon rite? how is it? I applied there

[]: Re: Symbolism: Did Kaoru break Shinji's heart?             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Jan 04, 2007 7:54 pm Reichu wrote: InvaderRen wrote: During that pause as Eva 01 holds Kaworu, a prog knife is clearly protruding from its chest, the heart perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 8:01 pm InvaderRen wrote: what are your opinions? and on a side note, Ornette, you go to Carnegie Mellon rite? how is it? I applied there

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 8:12 pm do you have any idea if the cheating is still that bad? sounds kinda "eh..." for a school like that

[]:             Quote Post by flatulant » Thu Jan 04, 2007 8:13 pm InvaderRen your avatar scares me.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 8:20 pm InvaderRen wrote: do you have any idea if the cheating is still that bad? sounds kinda "eh..." for a school like that

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 8:27 pm how about the campus and area?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 04, 2007 9:26 pm flatulant wrote: InvaderRen your avatar scares me.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 9:35 pm Me wrote: It doesn't quite look like the heart to me. It looks like it cut into the left shoulder armor.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 04, 2007 9:36 pm Sailor Star Dust: Erm, I didn't notice anyone agreeing with it...

[]:             Quote Post by flatulant » Thu Jan 04, 2007 9:37 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: flatulant wrote: InvaderRen your avatar scares me.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 05, 2007 6:02 pm [URL=http://img178.imageshack.us/my.php?image=eps24neuralfeedbackeh2.jpg] [/URL] Neural feedback reveals all.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Fri Jan 05, 2007 7:18 pm ok, so it didnt hit the heart directly, but the symbolism is still there, no?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 05, 2007 7:22 pm InvaderRen wrote: ok, so it didnt hit the heart directly, but the symbolism is still there, no?
